sections.title: What to test
items: Strict 2–5 word limit blocks Check when out of range

exercises.title: H: Word patterns (sentence transformation)
exercises.instructions: Complete each second sentence using the word given, so that it has a similar meaning to the first sentence. Write between two and five words.
exercises.studentInstruction: Use the keyword to complete the second sentence. Write 2–5 words only.

items.sentence:
Mia is very good at drawing portraits.
**TALENTED** Mia is really ...... portraits.

explanationContent.whyCorrect: Use the keyword "" unchanged to rewrite the sentence as "talented at drawing".
explanationContent.grammarFocus: Verb + preposition patterns (succeed in, capable of, talented at) are fixed — learn them as chunks.
explanationContent.wordCountMistake: Write between 2 and 5 words, including the keyword "" unchanged. Contractions such as don't count as one word.
explanationContent.commonMistake: Check that your answer keeps the keyword, matches the word limit, and preserves the meaning of the prompt sentence.
explanationContent.similarExample: Similar pattern: keep "" and rewrite the grammar to match the prompt meaning.
explanationContent.usefulTip: Count your words (2–5) before you check — include "" unchanged and read the completed second sentence aloud.

items.sentence:
The students were tired but didn't stop working.
**CONTINUED** The students were tired but they ...... their work.

explanationContent.whyCorrect: Use the keyword "" unchanged to rewrite the sentence as "continued with".
explanationContent.grammarFocus: Use "" exactly as given and change the grammar around it to preserve the meaning of the first sentence.
explanationContent.wordCountMistake: Write between 2 and 5 words, including the keyword "" unchanged. Contractions such as don't count as one word.
explanationContent.commonMistake: Check that your answer keeps the keyword, matches the word limit, and preserves the meaning of the prompt sentence.
explanationContent.similarExample: Similar pattern: keep "" and rewrite the grammar to match the prompt meaning.
explanationContent.usefulTip: Count your words (2–5) before you check — include "" unchanged and read the completed second sentence aloud.

items.sentence:
I have no information about this subject.
**KNOW** I ...... this subject.

explanationContent.whyCorrect: Use the keyword "" unchanged to rewrite the sentence as "don't know anything about".
explanationContent.grammarFocus: Use "" exactly as given and change the grammar around it to preserve the meaning of the first sentence.
explanationContent.wordCountMistake: Write between 2 and 5 words, including the keyword "" unchanged. Contractions such as don't count as one word.
explanationContent.commonMistake: Check that your answer keeps the keyword, matches the word limit, and preserves the meaning of the prompt sentence.
explanationContent.similarExample: Similar pattern: keep "" and rewrite the grammar to match the prompt meaning.
explanationContent.usefulTip: Count your words (2–5) before you check — include "" unchanged and read the completed second sentence aloud.

items.sentence:
Nobody can finish this project in ten minutes.
**CAPABLE** Nobody ...... this project in ten minutes.

explanationContent.whyCorrect: Use the keyword "" unchanged to rewrite the sentence as "is capable of finishing".
explanationContent.grammarFocus: Verb + preposition patterns (succeed in, capable of, talented at) are fixed — learn them as chunks.
explanationContent.wordCountMistake: Write between 2 and 5 words, including the keyword "" unchanged. Contractions such as don't count as one word.
explanationContent.commonMistake: Check that your answer keeps the keyword, matches the word limit, and preserves the meaning of the prompt sentence.
explanationContent.similarExample: Similar: He is capable of solving difficult problems.
explanationContent.usefulTip: Count your words (2–5) before you check — include "" unchanged and read the completed second sentence aloud.
